HEARING RE: WALMART, INC.'S MOTION FOR PROTECTIVE ORDER
Off Calendar Nov-15-2022 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

APPEARANCES (VIA ZOOM VIDEOCONFERENCE):
Atty. CLIFFORD A. CHANLER, Esq. of Chanler LLC (Tel: 203-722-4514) for plaintiff;
Atty. GREGORY S. BERLIN, Esq. of Alston & Bird (Tel: 213-576-1045) for defendant Walmart Inc.

HEARING RE: DEFENDANT WALMART INC.'S MOTION FOR PROTECTIVE ORDER.

Both sides stipulate to the matter being decided by the pro tem judge. The pro tem judge and counsel for both sides (all appearing remotely) authorize the clerk to sign the formal stipulation form on their behalf.

Matter argued and submitted.

The Court rules as follows: Defendant's motion is granted in part and denied in part.

The Court instructs counsel for plaintiff to draft a summary proposed order in Word format which attaches a protocol order that has been agreed to by both sides. The summary proposed order is to be emailed to the pro tem judge at rennepar85@gmail.com.

Judge Pro Tem: Paul Renne; Clerk: Sean Kane; Not Reported. (302/JPT)

Walmart Inc.'S Notice Of Motion And Motion For Protective Order; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities
Off Calendar Oct-25-2022 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES VIA ZOOM VIDEOCONFERENCE:
Clifford Chanler, Esq. (203.722.4514; clifford@chanlerllc.com) of Chanler LLC for Plaintiff.
Gregory Spallas, Esq. (415.278.9400; gspallas@psalaw.net) of Phillips Spallas & Angstadt LLP for Defendant.
Gregory Berlin, Esq. (213.576.1045; greg.berlin@alston.com)of Alston & Bird as Co-counsel for Defendant.

A R G U E D. The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument vacates the tentative ruling and rules as follows:

DEFENDANT WALMART INC'S Motion For Protective Order: in light of the trial date having been continued, there is no longer a timing concern, parties may bring the issues before Judge Pro Tem Renne for clarification on their questions and to work out the issues for seeking more time to produce the documents.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer Jr., Clerk: S. Le, Not Reported. =(302/RBU)
